workload_description: Queueing/admission probe: piecewise-constant open-loop rate - 60 s base at 2 rps, then a 15 s burst at 20 rps (10x amplitude), repeating every 75 s for eight cycles. Arrivals stay open-loop within each phase, so queue growth during bursts is fully visible (no coordinated omission). Exercises admission control, shedding, and Retry-After behavior at the gateway. Chat-like lengths, output capped at 512 tokens. Canonical suite v1 (IB-T003).
note: provisioned_mu8: long-run average offered rate (5.6 rps) < service rate (8 rps) -- system is stable overall; the burst phase alone briefly exceeds capacity but the queue drains within each 60s baseline window (see tests/dynamics/test_simulator.py::test_burst_decay_back_to_steady_state_after_the_burst_ends). underprovisioned_mu3: service rate (3 rps) below the long-run average offered rate (5.6 rps) -- unstable, in-system count ratchets upward cycle over cycle with no recovery.
